Close the chat.
Your “automation” dies.

That’s how most AI tools still work.

I built a network of Wingmans on Emergent:
each one assigned a job, running on schedules and triggers, handling the small work that quietly eats your headspace all day.

Watch this 👇 https://t.co/Os9OBQkfdv

Here’s what’s actually happening in that demo:

→ Each Wingman runs on a schedule or fires on a trigger
(new email, calendar update, inbound message)

→ Low-stakes tasks: it executes automatically

→ Consequential actions: it pauses, asks once, then acts

That’s the part that

The work exhausting you usually isn’t the big task.

It’s the pile of small ones constantly sitting in your head:
follow-ups, reminders, nudges, updates, checks.

Wingman removes that layer.

That’s when AI stops being interesting and starts being useful.

Try it 👇
